description: Phase 3 gameplay relationship graph connecting DataByteSprites Studio data, runtime owners, source files, species, lore, and future systems.

graphModel.direction: Edges generally read as source edgeType target. For example, dd-capture-runtime owns capture-flow.

nodes.label: DataByteSprites Game Data Manifest
nodes.description: Shared manifest for game data, runtime scripts, items, encounter pools, and validation targets.

nodes.label: DataByteSprites Species
nodes.description: Studio species metadata, stats, lore hooks, discovery hooks, and capture metadata.

nodes.label: DataByteSprites Moves
nodes.description: Studio move index for battle actions.

nodes.label: DataByteSprites Type Chart
nodes.description: Type effectiveness and capture bonus rules.

nodes.label: DataByteSprites Abilities
nodes.description: Ability records reserved for future ability runtime.

nodes.label: Source Files Index
nodes.description: Source ownership, runtime owners, legacy references, and future module targets.

nodes.label: Lore Index
nodes.description: Story, artifacts, events, rules, and world canon records.

nodes.label: Scanner Shell
nodes.description: Permanent scanner entry and page shell for Data Discovery.

nodes.label: Encounter Discovery
nodes.description: Signal-based discovery loop and encounter generation.

nodes.label: Capture Flow
nodes.description: Capture odds, ByteCoin use, and success/failure rules.

nodes.label: Capture Result Screen
nodes.description: Capture result display owned by Product App v3.

nodes.label: Battle System
nodes.description: Battle helper hooks, type utilities, enemy move selection, and combat events.

nodes.label: DataByteDex Progress
nodes.description: Seen/captured progress shared by Product App v3 and DataByteDex.

nodes.label: Party Management
nodes.description: Current party and lead sprite state inside Product App v3 until split into a future module.

nodes.label: Inventory Runtime
nodes.description: Current item state inside Product App v3 until split into a future item runtime.

nodes.label: databyte-discovery.html
nodes.description: Boots the Phase 3 runtime chain.

nodes.label: dd-canon-roster.js
nodes.description: Public 52-sprite roster bridge.

nodes.label: dd-studio-data-bridge.js
nodes.description: Loads Studio manifest, species, moves, and type chart data.

nodes.label: dd-gameplay-rules-2-4.js
nodes.description: Gameplay rules for capture odds, caps, stability, and move tuning.

nodes.label: dd-capture-runtime.js
nodes.description: Phase 3 capture runtime owner.

nodes.label: dd-encounter-runtime.js
nodes.description: Phase 3 encounter runtime owner.

nodes.label: dd-battle-engine-2-4.js
nodes.description: Phase 2.4/3 battle helper owner.

nodes.label: dd-battle-balance-2-4.js
nodes.description: Battle and capture balance layer that defers to gameplay rules.

nodes.label: databyte-discovery-product-app-v3.js
nodes.description: Functional Product App v3 UI orchestrator.

nodes.label: databytedex-shared-renderer.js
nodes.description: DataByteDex shared roster renderer.

nodes.label: studio-runtime-bridge-checks.js
nodes.description: Runtime health checks for the Product App chain.

nodes.label: dd-health-signal-bridge.js
nodes.description: Compatibility health/signal display bridge.

nodes.label: dd-scan-bg.js
nodes.description: Scanner background visual layer.

nodes.label: databyte-discovery-product-app.js
nodes.description: Former Phase 2 Product App retained for reference.

nodes.label: databyte-standalone-app.js
nodes.description: Former Scanner OS runtime owner retained for behavior recovery.

nodes.label: dd-battle-os-v2.js
nodes.description: Former Scanner OS battle owner retained for behavior recovery.

nodes.label: Leovolt
nodes.description: Rogue starter and first-capture sprite.

nodes.label: Screensavior Line
nodes.description: Screensavior to Monitor Man screen-guardian line.

nodes.label: DisplayDevil Line
nodes.description: DisplayDevil to Mirror Master reflection/trickster line.

nodes.label: NullKing
nodes.description: Locked Null-aligned future boss seed.

nodes.label: DataByte Coin
nodes.description: Capture artifact that traps and stabilizes sprites.

nodes.label: Null Signal Origin
nodes.description: Inciting event that causes sprites to manifest.

nodes.label: First Capture
nodes.description: Vic captures Leovolt, proving DataByteSprites can be studied.

nodes.label: Screens and Signal Manifestation
nodes.description: Screens and signal anomalies act as manifestation gateways.

nodes.label: XP Rewards
nodes.description: Future reward and XP runtime.

nodes.label: Progression and Scanner Rank
nodes.description: Future rank and scanner progression runtime.

nodes.label: Journal
nodes.description: Future discovery journal/log system.

nodes.label: Missions
nodes.description: Future scanner objectives and missions.

nodes.label: Asset Library
nodes.description: Studio asset catalog referenced by species and source indexes.

nodes.label: Simulation Lab
nodes.description: Future Studio sandbox for mechanics, AI workflows, and world simulations.

edges.reason: The page boots the public roster first.
The page boots the Studio data bridge after the public roster.
The page boots shared gameplay rules before runtime owners.
The page boots the capture runtime before Product App v3.
The page boots the encounter runtime before Product App v3.
Product App v3 is the live UI orchestrator.
The bridge loads the game data manifest.
Species data overlays the public roster.
Move data is exposed to runtime systems.
Type chart data is exposed to battle runtime systems.
The bridge overlays Studio records onto the public roster.
Encounter Runtime owns signal generation and encounter tuning.
Encounter Runtime reads encounter pools from the game data manifest.
Encounter Runtime picks playable species from the shared roster.
Encounter Runtime tunes capture-related fields through Capture Runtime.
Capture Runtime owns capture odds, caps, rolls, and failed-capture bonuses.
Capture Runtime spends ByteCoins based on the lore artifact.
Capture Runtime defers baseline odds and caps to Gameplay Rules.
Battle Engine owns type utilities, event hooks, and enemy move helpers.
Battle Engine uses the Studio type chart.
Battle Engine works with the Studio move index.
Balance Runtime defers to shared Gameplay Rules.
Gameplay Rules define capture odds, caps, and stability baselines.
Gameplay Rules tune move power, accuracy, and stability effects.
Product App v3 owns the visible scanner UI.
Product App v3 displays capture and battle results.
Party state is currently handled inside Product App v3.
Inventory state is currently handled inside Product App v3.
Product App v3 calls Encounter Runtime for scanner results.
Product App v3 calls Capture Runtime for capture odds and attempts.
Product App v3 calls Battle Engine helpers for type and enemy move logic.
Shared renderer owns the public DataByteDex view.
DataByteDex renders the shared roster.
Species data enriches the shared runtime roster shown in Dex.
Lore records document species and world rules.
Leovolt is tied to the Null signal outbreak.
The first capture event centers on Leovolt.
The DataByte Coin enables the first capture.
Screen manifestation rules support the screen-guardian line.
Screen manifestation rules support the reflection line.
Product App v3 replaces the Phase 2 Product App as live owner.
Product App v3 replaces the older Scanner OS runtime owner.
Battle Engine replaces the older Scanner OS battle owner.
Future rewards will consume battle outcomes.
Future rank progression will depend on rewards.
Future journal will consume lore and discovery history.
Future missions will consume encounter events.
Future simulation lab will test battle outcomes.

orphanPolicy.description: Every edge source and target must match a node id in this graph. Legacy files are retained only when they have explicit replacement edges.
